channel.title: 中国日报-观点
channel.description: 中国日报-观点 RSS Feed

item.title: ISPO Beijing to kick off with fully booked halls
item.author: 中国日报-观点

item.title: Chinese consultants reveal achievements in rapid business growth
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Chinese consultants reveal achievements in rapid business growth

item.title: Holidays help us transcend differences
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Despite being a Muslim, I look forward to this time of year to share the jovial activities of Christmas and New Year's with my fellow human beings, regardless of their religious beliefs.

item.title: Media failed Wu in life and even in death
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Wu Yongning, known for scaling skyscrapers without safety equipment to snap astonishing selfies, died last month after slipping off a 62-story building in Changsha, Hunan province. The video of the 26-year-old's accidental fall went viral online about a month later.

item.title: Timely news on military will boost public confidence
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The official micro blog or weibo of the People's Liberation Army Air Force has sparked a heated discussion online by posting a piece of news saying the air force's planes have flown on routes and over areas which they had never flown before.

item.title: Party-to-party talks for mutual benefit
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The recent Communist Party of China in Dialogue with World Political Parties High-Level Meeting in Beijing, the first such dialogue between the CPC and political parties from across the world, was organized with some specific goals in mind.

item.title: US can't rein in DPRK by using force
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The international community is keeping a close eye on the Korean Peninsula situation as Republic of Korea President Moon Jae-in pays a four-day state visit to China, especially because Pyongyang's Korean Central News Agency termed the just concluded US-ROK military drill a "projected war rehearsal" that will push the already acute situation on the peninsula to "the brink of nuclear war".

item.title: Uphill battle of Chinese tea in Russia
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The Chinese tea in Russia is not a novelty. Tea from the Middle Kingdom first reached tsarist Russia in the 16th century via the ancient Silk Road. However, as history unfolded, the Indian tea gradually held sway over the market leaving its Chinese counterpart behind by offering lower prices and greater accessibility to tea products.

item.title: Exclusive Interview with the Head of UN Women China on Gender Equality and Gender-based Violence
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: On November 24th, UN Women jumpstarted the 2017 #OrangetheWorld campaign with a bang. The 'Orange the World' awareness campaign spans 16 days, wherein individuals and groups alike are encouraged to spread information and awareness about gender-based violence.

item.title: 'Orange the World': Enlightening Youths on the Reality of Gender Inequality
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The 'Orange the World' awareness campaign spans 16 days, wherein individuals and groups alike are encouraged to spread information and awareness about gender-based violence. Awareness about gender inequality and the violence so often perpetuated against women and girls is paramount in the fight to bring an end to these issues.

item.title: Exclusive interview with UN coordinator in China on gender equality
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development was a plan adopted by the United Nations world leaders from over 193 countries in September 2015. It officially came into effect in 2016 and has set a basic framework to the year 2030. The 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development spans over 17 goals that cover a variety of social problems such as health, education, water supply and sanitation.

item.title: UN Women and Netease announce the winners for the 2017 "Women's Media Award"
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: UN Women China and its prominent media partner Netease are awarding leading women in the media who have given notable contributions to the fastest growing industries in China.

item.title: Exaggerated medical ads need stricter regulation
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: "Drink our products and you can extend your life span by 10 percent." So said Li Chuyuan, board chairperson of Guangzhou Phar. Holdings (GPH), at the recently held Fortune 2017 Brainstorm Tech International, "That's the study result of the National 863 Plan."

item.title: Fake and shoddy drugs a threat to people, challenge for administrators
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The CFDA is also seeking to revise the countryâs Pharmaceutical Administration Law, by adding a clause stipulating that China should set up a professional team of national medicine inspectors to help ramp up safety control in the entire process of drug-making, selling and distribution.

item.title: Julia Gillard: World keen to learn from China's education experience
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Former Prime Minister of Australia, Julia Gillard, proclaimed global interest in boosting cooperation with China in the field of education and scientific research in an exclusive interview with China Daily.

item.title: Internet essential to ending poverty worldwide
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: After a period of world-historic poverty reduction, China faces a difficult "last mile" of progress on the road to ensure the end of poverty in the country by 2020.

item.title: Long overdue reform of the sports administration system is expected
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A REPORT PUBLISHED BY A MAGAZINE affiliated to the Supreme People's Procuratorate that exposes corruption in the sports administration departments of various levels has gained wide attention on the social media.

item.title: Better secure deposits for bike use
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: SHORT-HIRE BIKE GIANTS MOBIKE AND OFO have dismissed reports accusing the two of misappropriating the deposits riders pay to use their dockless, GPS-enabled Mobike and Ofo bikes.

item.title: Publicizing info of pedophiles helps protect kids
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: ON FRIDAY, a local court in Huaian city, East China's Jiangsu province, sentenced four men found guilty of raping and sexually abusing minors. Besides receiving their deserved legal penalties, the court also made their names and other personal information public and forbade them from taking any jobs in the future that would bring them into contact with children.

item.title: è¥ä¸ç¨åºæ­¢(<EM>yÃ­ngyÃ¨shuÃ¬ fÃ¨izhÇ</EM>): Business tax abolished
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The process of replacing business tax with value-added tax has now been completed, as the State Council, China's Cabinet, announced on Friday that the business tax that has been in place for 60 years in China has been abolished.

item.title: A hairy proposal
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A hairy proposal

item.title: How gas-for-coal program is carried out makes a difference
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The National Development and Reform Commission, China's top economic planner, has stepped in to coordinate the major natural gas suppliers to stabilize prices and ensure an adequate supply to cope with rising demand in northern China.

item.title: Australian leader should not pander to anti-China bias
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Turnbull should not have bought into this media orchestrated falsehood. And it is wrong for him to engineer his country's policies based on the Australian media's bias against China. The unjustified finger-pointing at China only hurts Australiaâs ties with its biggest trading partner.

item.title: WIC should address benefits, challenges of the internet
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The fourth World Internet Conference (WIC) opened Dec 3 in the ancient town of Wuzhen in Zhejiang, China. In this unassuming little ancient town, the past and future world meet together, history joins technology to fuse the present with the future.

item.title: Outdated virtues still being used to subjugate women
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: "WHEN YOUR HUSBAND beats you, enjoy it. When he uses dirty words against you, tolerate it. You are a female and you should stay at the bottom of society." These are quotes from a so-called female virtue training agency in Liaoning province.

item.title: Fraud may have resulted in manslaughter
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: MORE THAN 1,000 CANCER PATIENTS in Nanjing, East China's Jiangsu province, reportedly invested in a local company promising an annualized return rate of 13 percent and funding for a recovery program.

item.title: US violates WTO rules, breaks pledge
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: On Dec 1, the United States formally informed the World Trade Organization that it opposes granting China market economy status. This breaches its promise that China would automatically be granted market economy status by 2016.

item.title: Insurance reform to boost public welfare
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The State Council, China's Cabinet, recently decided to transfer 10 percent equity shares of State-owned enterprises and financial institutions to the National Council for Social Security Fund.

item.title: EU welcome to work with China to help Africa
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Nearly six out of 10 Africans today are below the age of 25 and by the middle of this century, one in four people in the world will be an African. But last year, about 5,000 migrants, mainly Africans, drowned in the Mediterranean on their way to seek shelter in Europe.

item.title: Brexit deal
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Brexit deal

item.title: Right for companies to pay attention to social justice and poverty relief
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: ALIBABA, China's e-commerce giant, said it will invest 10 billion yuan ($1.51 billion) for poverty alleviation over the next five years.

item.title: èæè´¢äº§ç»§æ¿(<EM>xÅ«nÇ cÃ¡ichÇn jÃ¬chÃ©ng</EM>): Virtual property inheritance
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A man living in Shenzhen in South China's Guangdong province has inherited his dead mother's cellphone number as "virtual property".

item.title: Heating policy inconsiderate
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Northern China has long been plagued by smoggy weather, especially in winter when heating is supplied, and they have a deep longing for cleaner air. That explains why residents in the region have warmly welcomed the top authorities' promotion of natural gas as a substitute for coal.

item.title: Alarm bells ringing as US continues to up the stakes
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: There is real danger in the game they are playing, as the bellicose posturing between the US and DPRK leaders is raising the stakes to the point where they are putting their own futures as leaders on the line. With both counting on the other to fold, that is high-stakes play that risks misjudgment and conflict, and puts regional stability in jeopardy.

item.title: Wuzhen is building a strong foundation for the Iinternet's future
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: In the early days of the internet during the late 1960s, the technology wasnât purely meant for a worldwide access. It existed among a few interconnected networks in the United States and later joined by others in Europe. Later on came the World Wide Web (www), of which credit is given to Sir Tim Berners-Lee.

item.title: Are tax cuts a US supply-side scam?
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Tax cuts masquerading as tax reform are the best way to describe the thrust of Washingtonâs latest policy gambit. The case is largely political â namely, the urgency of a Republican Congress to deliver a legislative victory for a Republican president. The consequences, however, are ultimately economic â and, unsurprisingly, likely to be far worse than the politicians are willing to admit.

item.title: Wuzhen Summit
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Wuzhen Summit

item.title: Wuzhen Summit
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Wuzhen Summit

item.title: Internet conference provides venue for exchanging ideas on digital era
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: China is now home to about 750 million netizens and has the world's second-largest digital economy. It is also in an advantageous position in developing e-commerce and third-party mobile payments.

item.title: Disorder in Trump administration persists
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: It is sad but true that the world may have to wait longer for the disorder to end.

item.title: Rights to those with HIV has to be protected
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A Beijing security guard was reportedly told to leave his post after being diagnosed with the virus, and some 15 students with HIV, who had to sit their college entrance examinations in a separate room in June, have fallen victim to discrimination since entering college.

item.title: CPC continues to make history
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: These anniversaries serve as milestones to look back on what has been achieved over the past decades and highlight that the bright prospects for the Party and the country in the new era cannot be attained without reform, innovation and determination.

item.title: Building a better world for all is China's mission
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Xi, as leader of the world's largest party, is calling for all political parties to join hands in building a community of shared future to benefit all mankind.

item.title: Japan historians add insult to the wounds of comfort women
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Osaka Mayor Hirofumi Yoshimura has vowed to snap his city's six-decade sister-city relationship with San Francisco in protest against the US city accepting a statue of "comfort women" as public property.

item.title: Trade with China amid NAFTA friction
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: From China's standpoint, a bilateral deal with Canada would open new doors to North America and pave the way to the Free Trade Agreement of Asia Pacific, which would be broader and more inclusive than the current trade pact efforts.

item.title: Digital economy set to play greater role
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: With its theme of "Developing Digital Economy for Openness and Shared Benefitsï¼Building a Community of Common Future in Cyberspace", the Fourth World Internet Forum in Wuzhen, Zhejiang province, has attracted worldwide attention

item.title: 16+1åä½ (<EM>16+1 hÃ©zuÃ²</EM>): 16+1 cooperation
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: On Nov 27, Premier Li Keqiang attended the sixth meeting of the leaders of China and 16 Central and Eastern European countries in Budapest, Hungary.

item.title: Building of a better world for all is Chinaâs mission
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Bright prospects for the commons are what General Secretary Xi Jinping of the Communist Party of China Central Committee sought to convey in his keynote speech at the world political parties dialogue on Friday, in which he accentuated his proposal that countries work together to build a community of shared future for all mankind.

item.title: Daniel Franklin's book Megatech explores how technology will influence our lives in 2050
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Based on the idea that it can be useful to consider the longer term, it encourages us to be more thoughtful about the technologies we are building and their implications on our world, three decades from today.

item.title: Building a better world with foreign parties
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Serving the Chinese people has always been the primary goal of the Communist Party of China, but it has also had a global vision, and the ongoing CPC in Dialogue with World Political Parties High-level Meeting in Beijing is an example of giving shape to that vision.

item.title: US move threatens free trade
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The legal brief the United States filed with the World Trade Organization on Thursday to oppose the designation of China as a market economy risks derailing Sino-US economic relations and reflects Washington's unwillingness to respect free trade and globalization.

item.title: Sway of Chinese language on display
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: At a recent forum in Hong Kong, Jim Rogers, a Wall Street tycoon, played for the other participants a video of his daughter reciting aloud a classical Chinese poem. This is not the first time Happy Rogers, the elder daughter of the global investor, has exhibited her proficiency in the Chinese language.

item.title: Using professionals for cities' benefits
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Some second-tier cities are competing with not only each other, but also first-tier cities to attract talents. The governments of some second-tier cities are offering professionals local hukou (household registration), which is linked to all social welfare benefits, residential accommodation and cash subsidies.

item.title: Argentina hopes China will help improve WTO
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Argentina counts on China's commitment to the multilateral system to achieve the objective of reaffirming and improving the WTO.

item.title: Who has the right of way on zebra crossings?
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Since June, Beijing traffic police have begun enforcing a fine of 200 yuan ($29) and deduction of three demerit points for motorists who fail to give way to pedestrians on crosswalks. Do pedestrians always have priority on crossroads? Forum readers share their opinions.

item.title: Death and taxes may be certain, but a tax on dying certainly isn't
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A MEMBER OF the National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference, the top political advisory body, recently proposed amending the inheritance tax.

item.title: Jilin's tourism move breaks the law
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: TOURIST SITES with a 4A rating or above in Northeast China's Jilin province will reportedly charge visitors only half the ticket prices as long as they are registered residents of the Yangtze River Delta, the Hong Kong and Macao special administrative regions and Taiwan, for a certain period of time.

item.title: Dialogues will reduce initiative's risks
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The vision of China's Belt and Road Initiative evokes the ancient trading networks along the Silk Road and Maritime Silk Road, encompassing more than 70 countries and reaching beyond, connecting China's transport and communications infrastructure network across Central, South Asia, and Southeast Asia, the Middle East, Africa, Russia and Eastern Europe.

item.title: Party-to-party talks for shared future
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Leaders of more than 200 parties and political organizations from over 120 countries are taking part in a high-level dialogue with the Communist Party of China's officials in Beijing from Nov 30 to Dec 3. The record number of parties and organizations will exchange their experiences with the CPC and discuss the latter's concept of building a community of shared future for mankind.

item.title: Food trucks offer food for thought
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: In New York, including glitzy downtown and midtown Manhattan, thousands of food trucks sell delicacies from around the world, from jianbing (Chinese crepe), hot dog, falafel, tacos, sushi and waffles to steaks, burgers, juices and desserts.

item.title: Efforts of all needed to improve kindergartens
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The State Council, China's Cabinet, has dispatched a team of inspectors across the country to check teachers' ethical standards and working styles. The move comes after recent media reports on "child abuse" at an RYB Education New World Kindergarten in Beijing.

item.title: Guizhou through a photographer's eye
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: For Bruce Connolly a visit to Guizhou provided an opportunity to capture images of this beautiful land and also reflect about rural life and the area's future.

item.title: Palace Museum's move can boost cultural confidence
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Shan Jixiang, head of the Palace Museum, said they are working to open more areas within the museum to tourists.

item.title: åææ¿å å°ç¡®æ(<EM>nÃ³ngcÅ«n chÃ©ngbÄodÃ¬ quÃ¨quÃ¡n</EM>): Confirmation and registration of the contracts for rural land use rights
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A Ministry of Agriculture official said at a news conference on Wednesday that the pilot program for the confirmation and registration of the contracts for rural land use rights has proceeded smoothly over the past two years.

item.title: US anti-dumping investigations go against fair trade
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: US anti-dumping investigations go against fair trade

item.title: Practical environmental measure
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The general offices of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China and the State Council, China's Cabinet, recently published a document stipulating that officials must receive an audit of natural resources when leaving office.

item.title: US Commerce Department's move goes against fair trade
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: By launching probes into the Chinese imports without any requests from US companies and industries, Washington is going against the free, fair and reciprocal trade that it has so often proclaimed it wants to safeguard.

item.title: Good kindergartens need efforts of all
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: <FONT face="Times New Roman">The State Council, China's cabinet, has dispatched a team of inspectors across the country to check teachers' ethical standards and working styles. The move comes after recent media reports on "child abuse" at an RYB Education New World Kindergarten in Beijing.</FONT>

item.title: Every reason for China, US to work together
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: China-watchers in Washington have lamented the lack of a presidential speech on China for some time. So the Brookings Institution invited seven scholars last week and asked them to put themselves in the shoes of US National Security Advisor H.R. McMaster and prepare such a speech.

item.title: Head transplant against existing law
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Italian surgeon Sergio Canavero announced on Nov 17 that a team led by him and Ren Xiaoping, a professor at Harbin Medical University, had transplanted the head of a corpse on the body of another, and hinted at carrying out the same operation on a living human.

item.title: SCO meeting to showcase effective diplomacy
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The Belt and Road Initiative could offer an opportunity to enhance the cohesiveness of the group, as all member states are on the Belt and Road routes and most of them have shown great interest in participating in the connectivity-centered projects.

item.title: Ikea displays its double standard
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Ikea should stop unequally treating Chinese consumers and extend its latest recall of defective chests of drawers to the Chinese market to show it attaches the same concern to the safety of Chinese consumers as it does to consumers in North America.

item.title: Grave concerns that DPRK crisis is getting out of hand
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Unilateral countermoves by individual stakeholders have proven insufficient, and ineffective, in reining in the nuclear weapons program of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, as its latest missile test on Wednesday demonstrated.

item.title: Land-based Aegis anti-missile defense
item.author: 中国日报-观点

item.title: Party set to realize Chinese Dream
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Following the 18th National Congress of the Communist Party of China in 2012, General Secretary Xi Jinping advanced the Chinese Dream as a guiding thought of governing the country. And the 19th Party Congress last month made "Xi Jinping Thought on Socialism with Chinese Characteristics for a New Era" a long-term guiding political theory for the Party to follow.

item.title: Close rural-urban benefit gap to safeguard migrants' rights
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The fact that most migrant workers receive medical treatment entirely at their own expense points to several loopholes in the nationwide medical insurance system. As of last year, there were more than 282 million migrant workers in cities across the country.

item.title: Local govts should cool housing market
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The number of personal loans more than tripled year-on-year from January to October, while the growth in retail sales of consumer goods dropped, suggesting that much of the loan money has entered the housing market.

item.title: Patent office has not been duped by fans
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Imagine 15 million people waving fans in the same direction at the same time... That idea appeared on the official website of the State Intellectual Property Office, as a "solution" to free Beijing of smog, because the person applying for a patent argues that so many people waving fans at the same time will produce a wind strong enough to blow smoggy weather away.

item.title: Grave concerns that DPRK crisis is getting out of hand
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: There is a severe trust deficit among the relevant parties that is being repeatedly exacerbated by the actions of Washington and Pyongyang. This, as Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Wednesday, is moving the situation further away from the point where a settlement of the crisis can begin.

item.title: 'Silk Road Dialogues' deepens UDNP, Himalayan Consensus Institute cooperation
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The United Nations Development Program China Office and Himalayan Consensus Institute, an environmental think tank, entered into a memorandum of understanding November 28, 2017, for the establishment of the Silk Road Dialogues, aiming at building resilience and advancing sustainable development in countries participating in the Belt and Road Initiative.

item.title: Snowflake generation struggling with university norms
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Universities. For hundreds of years, they have been places to debate new and exciting ideas, even when those ideas have been highly controversial or offensive to those outside the cloistered walls of academia.

item.title: Can 15 m people blow away smog? That's a joke
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Imagine 15 million people waving fans in the same direction at the same time

item.title: The Zunyi Conference rebooted
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: An account of the history of the Communist Party of China (CPC) or to be precise modern Chinaâs history would be incomplete without a reflection on the historical Long March, a march that was the turning point of the Communist forces (Red Army) on a courageous journey to victory against the Nationalist forces during the Chinese revolution and the total liberation of China.

item.title: Beijing Party chief hits nail on the head
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: THE NOV 18 FIRE at a cold storage basement in a building in Beijing's Daxing district, which claimed 19 lives, was reportedly caused by faulty wiring and flammable materials. After an investigation, 20 people have been detained by the police. Beijing Daily commented on Tuesday:

item.title: Time to bring down the curtain on animal shows
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: ON NOV 22, a senior citizen in Nanyang, Henan province, was bitten by a tiger shut in the cage of a circus while trying to feed it. On Saturday, another tiger wounded two children in Hongdong county, Shanxi province. Thepaper.cn comments:

item.title: Egypt attack related to political turmoil
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Editor's note: On Friday, terrorists bombed a mosque and then opened indiscriminate fire on the worshippers on Sinai Peninsula in Egypt, killing at least 305, including 27 children, and wounding 128. It was the deadliest in Egypt's modern history. Two experts share their views with China Daily's Zhang Zhouxiang. Excerpts follow:

item.title: Think twice about imposing property tax
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Huang Qifan, vice-chairman of the economic and finance committee of the National People's Congress, the country's top legislature, said at a recent forum that the imposition of residential property tax is imperative, listing four major advantages of the move. Before that, in November 2013, the Third Plenum of the 18th Communist Party of China Central Committee had said legislation on real estate tax will be accelerated, and the tax reform will be promoted in time.

item.title: Gender bias is the result of twisted male mindset
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Will girls be in a more disadvantageous position in the college entrance exam, or gaokao, in the future?

item.title: Call for nationwide toilet revolution more than a matter of convenience
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: As an important part of urban and rural civilization, clean toilets have been in short supply in many Chinese communities for years due to historical reasons.

item.title: Real estate tax to help normal homebuyers
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Real estate tax should be promoted according to the principles of legislation, and the process should be carried forward gradually. Enacting real estate tax legislation is a lengthy process, and the authorities should prudently differentiate the tax levying methods on sold houses and houses to be sold in the future.

item.title: Sports venues shouldn't be political battlefields
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Recently, the Chinese under-20 men's soccer team received an insulting slap in the face during a friendly soccer match in Germany, a Chinese-language commentary in People's Daily argued, after the relaxing and friendly game was interrupted by protesters holding "Tibetan independence" flags.

item.title: å ¨çéè¦åä¸æåéäº§(<EM>quÃ¡nqiÃº zhÃ²ngyÃ o nÃ³ngyÃ¨ wÃ©nhuÃ yÃ­chÇn</EM>): Globally important agricultural heritage
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: At the meeting of the Scientific Advisory Group on Globally Important Agricultural Heritage Systems held at the headquarters of the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization in Rome on Friday, several Chinese sites were approved as globally important agricultural heritage.

item.title: Anger at online rumors
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Anger at online rumors

item.title: Subversion won't be tolerated
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The Yueyang Intermediate People's Court in Hunan province sentenced two men to seven years and five years in prison on Tuesday for subversion of State power.

item.title: 'Tiger' hunt shows Party is resolved to clean house
item.author: 中国日报-观点

This is not only of great strategic significance to national development, but will also bring valuable opportunities to the innovation and development of China's internet technology and internet industry.

item.title: Better pay and training to ensure tots entrusted to qualified carers
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Taking care of toddlers who are vulnerable to almost any hazard is not an easy job, because it requires great perseverance and patience. Entrusting kids to insufficiently trained carers is not just risky but also irresponsible.

item.title: Confidence in UK battered by Brexit
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Even more conspicuous and measurable will be the financial losses the UK will have to bear due to the relocation of the two agencies. The two EU agencies along with pharmaceutical enterprises and lobbyist groups have contributed handsomely to the UK's GDP over the past years.

item.title: Surnames should adhere to tradition and social norms
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Some say we live in a different age. True, but that only makes holding on to our traditional culture even more important.

item.title: Rules governing soccer ban political provocations
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The team only agreed to continue playing in the televised match against TSV Schott Mainz after the protesters took down the flags.

item.title: Beijing's positive response encouraging
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: It is hoped that Cai's words will help ensure law-based governance and put an end to all the controversy.

item.title: EU could learn some economic growth lessons from China
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The EU has enough reason to learn from some of the economic and development policies of China.

item.title: '16+1' invigorates China-Europe ties
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: China firmly supports the European integration process, and wants to see a united EU, a prosperous Europe, and a stable euro. In fact, these have been backed by China's concrete actions.

item.title: Ties with ROK improving, but cautiously
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Communication between China and the Republic of Korea has gradually improved, leading to their recent agreement to resolve the Terminal High Altitude Area Defense anti-missile system issue.

item.title: Witnessing the rapid transformation of Guizhou
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Traveling by train is often a good way to view a country, to look not just at the landscape but also the levels of development.

item.title: Should grads insist on staying in big cities?
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: With the population decentralization policy taking effect in cities, and competition in the job market becoming fiercer, it's time college graduates pondered whether or not they should look for a job in a metropolis. Three experts share their views on the subject with China Daily's Wu Zheyu.

item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The Supreme People's Court announced on Thursday that China has launched a national information-sharing platform for paroles and the commuting of sentences, which will be completed by the end of next year.

item.title: Mosque attack suggests IS aims to make Sinai Peninsula its new base
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Most of the previous attacks launched by the IS in Sinai have been targeted at the military forces there, but this time they attacked Sufis whom the group has branded heretics. They did so in order to clear any possible opposition.

item.title: Strict standards required to keep safe watch on kids
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Fortunately, there has been no major incident involving children's smart watches yet. We hope the responsible departments will draw up national standards as soon as possible to ensure that remains the case.

item.title: Tariff cuts to benefit more than just consumers
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: With lower tariff rates, people will pay less when buying these consumption goods. That's why the tax cut is widely praised as a move benefiting the people.

item.title: New era diplomacy to strengthen ties with CEE countries
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Premier Li Keqiang's visit to Hungary and participation in the Sixth China-Central and Eastern Europe Leaders' Meeting from Nov 26-29 are important diplomatic moves after the 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China, which will promote China-CEE cooperation in the framework of"16+1" mechanism.

item.title: Transient workers deserve respect
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A photo that went viral on social media platforms, captioned as homeless migrant workers sleeping outdoors on a roadside in the freezing cold of Beijing, which coincides with the city's 40-day crackdown on rental homes that are potential fire hazards, has been identified as one from the 2015 earthquake in Nepal.

item.title: Child abuse in kindergartens calls for tighter management
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The investigation into the RYB kindergarten scandal is still going on. It is hoped that the publication of the results will give the lie to the rumors rampant on the internet about the scandal. It is also hoped that the supervision and management of kindergartens will be effectively tightened nationwide for children to get the care they deserve.

item.title: Transient workers deserve respect
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A photo that went viral on social media platforms, captioned as homeless migrant workers sleeping outdoors on a roadside in the freezing cold of Beijing, which coincides with the city's 40-day crackdown on rental homes that are potential fire hazards, has been identified as one from the 2015 earthquake in Nepal.

item.title: Impression of a Guizhou trip with China Daily
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: I had the pleasure of being invited on a trip to Southwest China's Guizhou province with foreign and Chinese media experts and scholars to see the autumn beauty of Southwest China, as well as tread the road where China's greatest history was made. We learned a lot about the history of China and, visited Huamao and Gouba villages and Zunyi town.

item.title: Exploring Guizhou from an African viewpoint
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: I've had my fair share of travelling around China, and a recent week-long trip to Guizhou was my first time to the province. It was a true learning experience. I must admit, it is one of the most endowed regions in China in terms of natural beauty - fascinating environment with rich culture and history.

item.title: Power 'caged' for better governance
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A new item will be added to the list of tasks the State Council, China's Cabinet, should report to the Standing Committee of National People's Congress, the country's top legislature, as part of the latest efforts to place executive power under more scrutiny.

item.title: A country is defined by both might and culture
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Japan's imperial family has been catching the headlines in recent times. On Wednesday, Japan's Imperial Household Agency announced that 25-year-old Princess Mako, the eldest granddaughter of Emperor Akihito, will marry her college sweetheart Kei Komuro, a commoner, on Nov 4, 2018.

item.title: Transplants must be strictly supervised
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: On Nov 17, Italian surgeon Sergio Canavero announced that a team led by him and Ren Xiaoping, a professor at Harbin Medical University, had performed the first human head transplant on a corpse, and mentioned the prospect of doing such a surgical operation on a living human being.

item.title: Kindergarten âscandalâ shouldnât be a trust crisis for âgardenersâ

item.description: It's not the first time a âscandalâ involving a kindergarten has escalated into a big storm. Claiming the moral ground of protecting the youngsters, people are swarming to comment on social media, calling for harsh punishment for the âuglyâ actions of a Beijing-based RYB Education Kindergarten.

item.author: 中国日报-观点

item.author: 中国日报-观点

item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Xi's concept has emerged from the Party's long-term practice and historic struggle, and reflects the political wisdom and valuable experience of the CPC.

item.title: Dalian will always be my second hometown
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Most people complain that their once-spectacular city has since taken a turn for the worse over the years. People love to focus on the negative. Perhaps thatâs just part of human nature. On the contrary, my report on Dalian happens to be quite the opposite. So if youâre looking to read another venting session from another downer of a foreigner in China, you can stop reading right now.

item.title: Share the noble joys of Thanksgiving with your American friends
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The American Thanksgiving transcends distance and all other differences to bring members of American families and their friends together to share sumptuous meals and offer thanks to God for the blessings that were generously bestowed upon them.

item.author: 中国日报-观点

item.title: Importance of cultural development
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: President Xi Jinping met more than 600 individuals selected for their outstanding work promoting the country's cultural and ethical progress at a ceremony to honor them in Beijing on Friday.

item.title: Spending less time on internet could reduce depression
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Limiting screen time to one or two hours a day, as the university's professor of psychology Jean Twenge said, would statistically fall into the safe zone for device usage.

item.title: 19th Party Congress a vision for the future
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: At the recent "International Think-Tank Symposium, the 19th CPC National Congress: Implications for China and the World", some foreign scholars presented their views on the impact of the 19th Party Congress.

item.title: Systemic change to boost anti-graft fight
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China approved some amendments to the Party Constitution, including the Party disciplinary inspection system. Du Zhizhou, deputy director of the Clean Governance Research Center at Beihang University, explains to China Daily's Zhang Zhouxiang the importance of those amendments for society.

item.title: Japanese lesson to improve manufacturing
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Chinese electronics giant Hisense announced on Tuesday that it would acquire a 95 percent stake in Japan's Toshiba Visual Solutions Corp as part of its efforts to expand its global business. Under the 12.9 billion yen ($112.44 million) deal, which is likely to be completed by the end of February next year, Hisense Electric Co, the listed unit of Qingdao-based Hisense Group, will buy the TV business of Japan's struggling conglomerate Toshiba. After the transaction, Hisense will integrate the research and development wings, supply chains and global resource channels of the two companies.

item.title: Bonn offers scant relief from climate change
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Not much was expected to come out of the UN Climate Change Conference in Bonn, Germany, after US President Donald Trump pulled out of the Paris Agreement.

item.title: Danger on dark side of the 'City of Light'
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Paris is one of the world's biggest tourist destinations. Millions of people want to follow in the footsteps of writers such as Ernest Hemingway and Jack Kerouac and visit the "City of Light", cafes, romance, the Eiffel Tower and the Champs-Elysees.

item.title: China's solution for global governance
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The phrase "building a community of shared future for mankind", which President Xi Jinping has been frequently referring to both at home and abroad, reflects not only China's global governance philosophy, but also its determination to help build an equal, open and peaceful world.

item.title: Bauhinia valley green entrepreneurship camp kicks off
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The Bauhinia Valley Green Entrepreneurship Camp, held by the University of International Business and Economics and the Bauhinia Valley Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Center, launched on Nov 16, 2017.

item.title: Chinese home shoppers looking at US tech hubs
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: For the past three centuries, global investment has been and continues to be a hallmark of international powers. In the late 19th century and early 20th century, the United Kingdom and Western Europe led the world in investing their resources abroad. By the middle of the 20th century, the United States led the world in overseas investment.

item.title: China's progress: Could it happen in the US?
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Last month, I had the opportunity to attend the Five Years On exhibition with several of my colleagues, as well as foreign media and diplomats from around the world. The exhibit, running before, during and after the 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China, sets out to detail the achievements of the Chinese people over the last five years.

item.title: Growing global influence
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The diplomatic activities of President Xi Jinping after the 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China, from meeting visiting Russian Prime Minister Dmitry Medvedev and US President Donald Trump to attending the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation Economic Leaders' Meeting and paying state visits to Vietnam and Laos, have opened a new chapter in China's diplomacy and enhanced China's global influence, appeal and charisma.

item.title: Convergence remains on approach to DPRK issue
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: After US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that China agreed that the Democratic People's Republic of Korea cannot just freeze its nuclear weapons program in exchange for benefits, China clarified on Thursday that this was not a change to its position that its "dual suspension" proposal is the best "first step" for defusing the crisis.

item.title: US politicians misleading public with China bashing
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: At a recent China town hall held by the New York-based National Committee on US-China Relations, Susan Rice, the former US national security advisor, said China remains the most reliable whipping boyï¼after probably Hillary Clinton and Susan Rice herself.

item.title: Dealing with the homework pandemic
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Believe it or not, homework, not smog or housing prices, is causing nervous breakdowns for Chinese parents. Chinese social media are full of stories about neighbors hearing parents yelling while helping their children with homework.

item.title: Time to stop the casual use of antibiotics
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: This week is Antibiotic Awareness Week, and its main message is that people should seek expert advice before using antimicrobials.

item.title: Are private schools selling pipe dreams to students' parents?
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: During one of my journeys to Montreal, my hometown in Canada, I had the chance to meet a Chinese who was supposed to be studying in Canada, yet he was working at the counter of a Subway outlet in downtown Montreal.

item.title: <A title="" href="http://bbs.chinadaily.com.cn/thread-1889427-1-1.html" target=_blank>Do you think the general level of English proficiency has been on the rise in China?</A>
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: <A title="" href="http://bbs.chinadaily.com.cn/thread-1889427-1-1.html" target=_blank>Do you think the general level of English proficiency has been on the rise in China?</A>

item.title: Let court decide studentâs murder case
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Jiang Ge, a 24-year-old postgraduate student, was killed outside her apartment in Tokyo over a year ago after she was reportedly attacked by Chen Shifeng while trying to defend his former girlfriend Liu Xin from him. Jiangâs mother is seeking the death penalty for Chen, whose trial begins in Japan next month, and has accused Liu of being indifferent to the sacrifice of Jiang, sparking a heated online debate.

item.title: Consensus and initiative to prevent crisis and conflict in the Himalayas
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: In this era of global volatility, probability of crisis and conflict is further increased by the negative impact of climate change, environmental stress due to poor management of natural resources and limited economic prospects affecting the livelihoods of communities. One region where this is observed is the Himalayas. Water shortages, sudden changes in climatic conditions affecting agriculture, create conditions that can lead to tensions affecting several countries that share complex borders and trans-border eco-systems.

item.title: ä¸äºç»æµå ±åä½(dÅngyÃ <EM>jÄ«ngjÃ¬ gÃ²ngtÃ³ngtÇ</EM>):East Asian Economic Community
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Premier Li Keqiang advanced building an East Asian Economic Community in order to promote regional integration and common development at the 20th Association of Southeast Asian Nations, China, Japan and Republic of Korea (10+3) leaders' meeting in the Philippines on Tuesday.

item.title: The family career balance
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The family career balance

item.title: The family career balance
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The family career balance

item.title: Court's verdict will be based on evidence, not public opinion
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: JIANG GE, a 24-year-old Chinese student, was killed in the corridor outside the door of her apartment in Tokyo one year ago. She was reportedly attacked by a Chinese student surnamed Chen, while trying to defend his ex-girlfriend, Liu Xin. More than 1.5 million people have signed a petition supporting Jiang's mother who is seeking the death penalty in Chen's trial.

item.title: Company's sexist ads offensive and illegal
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: "IT IS JUICY. Do you want it?" says the text of an advertisement displaying the image of a young woman in bed, her feet chained. The advertisement of Hunan Juewei, a domestic fast food chain company, has provoked a storm of criticism.

item.title: Rules for return of hire-on-demand deposits required
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: SOME COMPANIES providing hire-on-demand bicycles are in so much trouble that they fail to respond to the requests users make via the companies' apps for the return of their deposits. This has resulted in users paying others to visit the offices of bike-sharing companies to get their deposits back.

item.title: Greater contributions
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: In his keynote speech at the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation CEO meeting, President Xi Jinping mapped out rosy prospects for China's benign interactions with the world in the new era and said China is willing to work harder with other parties to build an open, inclusive, clean and beautiful world with lasting peace and common security and prosperity.

item.title: RCEP must be made a reality at earliest date
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Five years have passed since the idea of the Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership was first raised in 2012, but negotiations are yet to conclude on the mega-trade pact involving the 10 member states of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations and China, South Korea, Japan, Australia, New Zealand and India.

item.title: Diplomacy in full swing in new era
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The leadership of the Communist Party of China has wrapped up its first series of diplomatic efforts after the 19th National Congress of the CPC, which included US President Donald Trump's maiden visit to China, President Xi Jinping's speech at the APEC Economic Leaders' Meeting in Vietnam, and Premier Li Keqiang's deliberations at the ASEAN+China (10+1) leaders' meeting in the Philippines.

item.title: Initiative set for bigger development role
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: After making significant headway in the past four years, the Belt and Road Initiative has entered the implementation stage and was even cited as a priority for further opening up the Chinese economy at the recent 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China.

item.title: The two engines of sustainable yet booming consumption
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: What started as a mere internet joke based on the day's "four-single-person" date in 2009 when Alibaba managed to register a one-day sales of 52 million yuan ($7.83 million) has this year helped the Chinese e-commerce giant to record sales of a jaw-dropping 168.2 billion yuan, 39.3 percent more than last year.

item.title: The dangers of online games
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The dangers of online games

item.title: Parents putting too much stress on their children
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A YOUNG CHILD in Shanghai was diagnosed with alopecia areata, or spot baldness, caused by too much stress. The two-and-a-half-year-old's mother sent him to five training classesï¼English, mathematics, fine arts, piano and public speakingï¼over the past two months in a bid to get him into a prestigious kindergarten next year.

item.title: TV company wrong to broadcast interview that may influence trial
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Jiang Ge, a 24-year-old Chinese student, was found dead with multiple stab wounds in the corridor of her Tokyo apartment more than a year ago, having reportedly fallen victim to a quarrel between her roommate and her roommate's ex-boyfriend. She was completing her postgraduate studies.

item.title: Square dancing rules set good example
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: ON MONDAY, the General Administration of Sport issued a detailed document to regulate square dancing, which forbids people square dancing in places such as martyrs' cemeteries or school grounds, while also requiring gymnasiums to allow square dancers access during certain hours.

item.title: A new era for human rights across the world
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: In the report he delivered at the opening of the 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China on Oct 18, General Secretary Xi Jinping said that as a result of the transformative changes that have been made during the past five years, the country is better prepared than ever before to take on the challenges in the new era.

item.title: Toward stronger China-South Africa ties
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: During the 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China, the leadership made two key judgments: the principal contradiction of China has evolved into one between unbalanced and inadequate development and the people's ever-growing needs for a better life; and socialism with Chinese characteristics has entered a new era.

item.title: 'Indo-Pacific' an 'America First' ploy
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Just before and during his maiden trip to Asia as US president, Donald Trump reiterated the importance of promoting a free and open "Indo-Pacific region", a term rarely used by his predecessors. Three experts share their views on Trump's "subtle shift" in language.

item.title: Elderly deserve better care and services
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: In his report to the 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China on Oct 18, General Secretary Xi Jinping said China will adopt policies that will help foster a social environment in which senior citizens are respected, cared for and live happily.

item.title: People-centric development
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The key to China's achievements over the past decades has been its leadership's understanding of the real needs of Chinese people. Increased fiscal inputs in education, healthcare, employment and other fields related to people's livelihoods, and efforts to promote an efficient and open market have enabled China's development fruits to be better shared among its people.

item.title: ç¢³å¸åº (<EM>tÃ n shÃ¬chÇng</EM>): Carbon market
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The 23rd Conference of Parties (COP 23) to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change commenced in Bonn, Germany, on Nov 6.

item.title: Right time for region to build economic community
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: By supporting each other in their pursuit of economic and social development, countries in the region can demonstrate they are not only good partners but also good neighbors, and in doing so forge a vibrant community with a shared future that can be a model for the world.

item.title: Smart family
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Smart family

item.title: Smart family
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Smart family

item.author: 中国日报-观点

item.title: Wider financial opening-up shows confidence in abilities
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: AT A NEWS CONFERENCE in Beijing on Friday, China's vice-finance minister said that China will increase the share ceiling for foreign investments in securities, capital, futures and asset management companies from 25 percent to 51 percent, and abolish the upper limit in three years.

item.title: Founders often give firms short life spans
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: ACCORDING TO ONE VETERAN ENTREPRENEUR, the average "shelf life" of a Chinese enterprise is about five to eight years, meaning that entrepreneurs must strive to improve their management as early as possible to break that cycle.

item.title: Sales records marred by too many gripes
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: THIS YEAR'S NOV 11 annual online shopping spree has ended, and one e-commerce platform after another has announced their sales reached record-high. Yet behind that there is hidden risk,

item.title: Further opening-up way forward
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: By integrating itself into the global economy, China has realized leapfrog development over the past nearly four decades since the reform and opening-up policy was launched.

item.title: China and ASEAN can safeguard stability in South China Sea
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Developments over the past few days reveal the broadening consensus among the members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations and China that they, and they alone, are ones who should get their maritime house in order, and they are fully capable of doing so.

item.title: EU could take a leaf out of China's book on diplomacy
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: This time last year, as the world was coming to terms with Donald Trump winning the US presidential election, Europeans and Chinese worried about the president-elect's provocative rhetoric and what he would do in office.

item.title: Xi-Trump talks will strengthen ties
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: US President Donald Trump will conclude his extended visit to Asia on Tuesday, during which he has visited Japan, the Republic of Korea, China, Vietnam and the Philippines. But of all the countries on his itinerary, his three-day visit to China has been the highlight of his first trip to Asia as US president.

item.title: Financial sector reforms get a shot in the arm
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Two recent but separate developments show the Chinese authorities' resolve to expedite measures to curb financial risks after the 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China

item.title: Transforming from imitators to innovators
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: E-commerce and e-payment, two of the âfour new inventions of Chinaâ, reached a new peak this year as the Singles Day sales on Nov 11 reached 253.97 billion yuan ($38.2 billion). Chinaâs ânew inventionsâ can be more accurately described as âinnovationsâ, because there have been similar inventions in foreign countries before.

item.title: Singles day discounts
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Singles day discounts

item.title: Singles day discounts
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Singles Day Discounts

item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Ecology of the internet of things

item.title: Singles Day sales record shows vitality of domestic consumption
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: SATURDAY MARKED the annual Nov 11 online shopping sales. The total amount generated in the sales this year is not yet known, but it will definitely set a record because within 13 hours it had exceeded 120.7 billion yuan ($18.1 billion), the total for the whole day last year.

item.title: Urgent to curb overuse of antibiotics in animals
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: ON NOV 7, the World Health Organization issued formal guidelines for the animal industries, which calls for food-animal producers to refrain from using antibiotics in healthy animals, so as to curb the rampancy of drug-resistant bacteria.

item.title: Shanghai sets good example for short-hire bikes
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: ON AUG 18, the Shanghai Transportation Commission calculated that the total number of short-hire bicycles in the city far exceeded the city's parking capacity, so it forbade enterprises from putting new ones into use and asked enterprises to maintain the existing ones well.

item.title: Ballast for long-term growth
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Premier Li Keqiang commented on the important role consumption plays in driving China's economic growth at a recent meeting with economic experts and entrepreneurs.

item.title: APEC leaders must now add substance to vision for growth
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Despite the lackluster global economic recovery, the Asia-Pacific has remained the world's most economically dynamic region and the main engine of global growth.

item.title: Japan-ROK rift means military alliance with US not possible
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Japan and the Republic of Korea, both US allies, watched closely how the other hosted US President Donald Trump when he visited the two countries last week. Compared with his one-night stay in Seoul, Trump's three-day visit to Tokyo gave Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe more time to tee off and talk with the US president.

item.title: Asia rising on mutually beneficial policy
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: There are signs that the Philippines-China relationship is among the priorities for both countries. This signifies not just the assistance (China is offering the Philippines), but also the trust it is gaining in doing so, the establishment of comfortable relations between China and the Philippines.

item.title: Trade essential for regional integration
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: While President Xi Jinping is on a state visit to Laos after attending the APEC Economic Leaders' Meeting and visiting Vietnam, Premier Li Keqiang is in the Philippines to take part in a series of high-level meetings across five days starting Sunday, including the 20th China-ASEAN (10+1) leaders' meeting and the 20th ASEAN-China, Japan and the Republic of Korea (10+3) leaders' meeting, and the 12th East Asia Summit.

item.title: China can help fight terrorism in SE Asia
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: At the 43rd Philippine Business Conference in October, Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte said the bullet that killed Isnilon Hapilon, Islamic State's "emir" in Southeast Asia, in Marawi was fired from a sniper rifle made in China.

item.title: Trump at APEC could spark regional shift
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: In a broader sense then, the US "rebalancing" and relative disengagement leaves the field open for other APEC members to contribute actively and substantively to APECâs work agenda.

item.title: China leading the way at APEC meeting
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: China has the experience, the technology, the companies, the human and financial resources and, most importantly, the will to contribute with APEC economies to solving the problem of building needed infrastructure.

item.title: Do foreigners find online shopping fun or pain?
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Due to its convenience, affordable prices and quick delivery, online shopping is increasingly making life easier for expats in China. As we count down to Singles Day, the world's largest shopping festival, our foreign readers share their online shopping stories with us.

item.title: Asia-Pacific's role vital to free trade
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The two-day 25th APEC Economic Leaders' Meeting started on Friday in Da Nang, Vietnam, at a time when globalization faces many challenges.

item.title: Tackle 'gray rhinos' before it's too late
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: With gray rhinos gathering speed, the world looks to China and other emerging economies for new and creative ideas to tackle the challenges ahead, and to push forward globalization and global governance.

item.title: Education quality, not name, defines a college
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Society has differentiated needs for higher education, so colleges should nurture and develop their special characteristics.

item.title: Preschools run by firms need strict supervision
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A video of a teacher throwing a girl's bag away in a kindergarten classroom and forcing kids to swallow mustard went viral on Tuesday.

item.title: The faces of Rugao
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: I spent a week in Rugao, Jiangsu, a third- or four-tier city about 200 km northwest of Shanghai, participating in the 20th Asian Masters Athletics Championship.

item.title: APEC looking at Xi for leadership
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: APEC leaders and the world would be watching what President Xi Jinping would bring to the meeting. There would be high expectation that the reinvigorated Xi would not be just another leader attending the meeting. In a way, the other APEC leaders would be looking to Xi to provide the leadership.

item.title: China will be more confident on Sino-US relations
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: US President Donald Trump arrived in China on a three-day state visit to meet with President Xi Jinping from Nov 8 to 10. The trip came soon after the 19th National Congress of Communist Part of China concluded.

item.title: US-China trade at a global crossroads
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Despite "America first" policies, President Trump's economic agenda needs expanding trade with China.

item.title: Sales of Japanese cars plunge
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Sales of Japanese cars plunge

item.title: Trump trip can pave the way for smooth ties ahead
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: US President Donald Trump clearly had a good time on Wednesday, the day he and his wife Melania Trump arrived in Beijing. In his tweets, he thanked China and its leaders for the "beautiful welcome" and "unforgettable afternoon and evening at the Forbidden City".

item.title: Region needs FTA for smoother trade
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: President Xi Jinping will attend the 25th APEC Economic Leaders' Meeting at Da Nang, Vietnam on Nov 10 and 11, and pay state visits to Vietnam and Laos from Nov 12 to 14.

item.title: Diplomacy will be more effective in new era
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The outcomes of the successful 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China have prompted some international observers to suggest China will become expansionist and may seek to lead the world. But they should rest assured that China's diplomacy will never become radical or adventurous.

item.title: Including financial onus better way to calculate spending ability
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: GUANGZHOU, capital of South China's Guangdong province, has said middle-income families can also apply to purchase low-income public housing

item.title: Laborers want more than just higher wages
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: MANY EXPRESS DELIVERY companies have raised the wages for employees in a bid to recruit more workers to cope with the huge number of deliveries resulting from the Nov 11 online shopping sales.

item.title: International claim too often rings hollow
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: AN INCREASING NUMBER of Chinese cities are hosting what they call international marathons.

item.title: Govt efforts boost Guizhou's growth
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Of the 27 provincial-level regions that recently published their gross domestic product growth figures for the first three quarters of this year, 22 registered a higher growth than the country's average, with Guizhou province and the Tibet autonomous region ranking first.

item.title: ç¦ç ¤åº(<EM>jÃ¬nmÃ©iqÅ«</EM>): No coal area
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Coal is the main source of air pollution in Beijing, Tianjin and Hebei province. So the construction of a "no coal area" has become the major task of the three regions.

item.title: APEC and the future of trans-Pacific free trade
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The 25th APEC Economic Leaders' Meeting, to be held in Da Nang, Vietnam, on Friday and Saturday, has elicited much international attention. It is the first APEC meeting hosted by Vietnam, as well as the first to be attended by US President Donald Trump, as part of the longest trip to the Asia-Pacific taken by a US president since 1992.

item.title: Sino-US relations stand at historic new starting point
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The concordant note struck by Xi and Trump showcased not only the personal rapport they have established, but also consolidated optimism about the prospects for bilateral ties, especially since Xi has previously emphasized the "leading role of head-of-state diplomacy", and Trump on Thursday touted the "chemistry" between himself and his host.

item.title: A railway that opened up southwest Guangdong
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: How the completion of a major railway project introduced Bruce Connolly to southwestern Guangdongâs stunning landscape and a fascinating river city.

item.title: Trump's visit: Expectations and predictions of experts
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Trump's visit: Expectations and predictions of experts

item.title: Countdown for annual e-shopping carnival
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Countdown for annual e-shopping carnival

item.title: Journalists still needed
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Wednesday marked the 18th Journalists' Day in China. It comes at a time, when journalists, traditionally the eyewitnesses and recorders of the times, are having to adapt to the enormous changes transforming the media.

item.title: Together Towards Prosperity and Harmonious Development
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The 25th anniversary APEC Economic Leaders' Meeting will take place in Danang very soon, on November 10 and 11.

item.title: èªç±è´¸ææ¸¯(<EM>zÃ¬yÃ³u mÃ oyÃ¬gÇng</EM>):Free trade ports
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: In the report he delivered at the opening session of the 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China, General Secretary Xi Jinping said more powers will be granted to pilot free trade zones to conduct reform and explore the opening of free trade ports.

item.title: Still hope US will join hands to combat climate change
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: With a new round of UN Climate Change Conference being held in Bonn, Germany, from Monday to Nov 17, and with US President Donald Trump visiting China from Wednesday through Friday, many people feel nostalgic about those days when Beijing and Washington took the lead in pushing for and formally signed the 2015 Paris Agreement, showing a good example of big powers joining hands to meet global challenges.

item.title: US, DPRK must meet halfway to end crisis
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: US President Donald Trump started his three-day visit to China from Wednesday at the invitation of President Xi Jinping. The international community is closely watching the two presidents' meeting, especially their discussion on the Korean Peninsula nuclear issue.

item.title: For US, business should matter
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The world watches with anticipation and some anxiety US President Donald Trump's state visit to China. How will the leaders of the world's largest and second-largest economies manage their bilateral relations amid an environment of global fragility and uncertainty?

item.title: Personal rapport can guide ties in new era
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: And it is wisdom and foresight that the leaders of the two countries are expected to show by providing strategic guidance for the development of relations during US President Donald Trump's three-day visit to China, which began on Wednesday.

item.title: The growth of China
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: On the 31st of August 2012, as I landed my feet on Chinese ground, I started to breathe in the air coming from the breeze. It was familiar. I felt so excited to be back in China, though that time was for my studies.

item.title: Are you planning to shop on the Singles' Day?
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Are you planning to shop on the Singles' Day?

item.title: Trump warms up to China for greater good
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: US President Donald Trump will meet President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Nov 8 during his first state visit to China, which is expected to further enhance the strategic understanding and friendship between the two countries and their heads of state, and hopefully elevate bilateral ties to a new level.

item.title: Money talks when business matters
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The world watches with anticipation and some anxiety US President Donald Trump's state visit to China. How will the leaders of the world's largest and second-largest economies manage their bilateral relations amid an environment of global fragility and uncertainty?

item.title: Local officials must have solid grasp of all environmental laws
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: ALTHOUGH A LAW ON THE PREVENTION and control of solid waste pollution has been in place for 22 years, the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress, China's top legislature, found that some local governments have no idea of the existence of such a law and bids to make people sort their garbage have repeatedly failed

item.title: Reusable delivery boxes could be cheaper, greener
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: THE ANNUAL SINGLES DAY shopping spree falls on Saturday. In order to avoid environmental pollution and save natural resources, several express delivery companies have opted for reusable plastic delivery boxes to replace the past one-time use boxes.

item.title: Cutting greenhouse gas emissions
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Cutting greenhouse gas emissions

item.title: Lead public to acceptance of guide dogs
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: A BLIND WOMAN was reportedly prevented from using the Beijing subway because her guide dog was not wearing a muzzle.

item.title: Supervising the supervisors
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The Standing Committee of the National People's Congress, China's top legislature, approved extending a pilot corruption supervision program nationwide as part of the vow to "put the supervisory power in the cage of institutions".

item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: In the report he delivered at the opening session of the 19th National Congress of the Communist Party of China on Oct 18, General Secretary Xi Jinping said that the development of a crime prevention and control system will be accelerated to combat and punish, in accordance with law, all illegal and criminal activities, such as pornography, gambling, drug abuse, gang violence, kidnapping and fraud, and to protect people's personal rights, property rights and right to dignity.

item.title: The world is now flat, at least, for consumers
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: Seeing is believing. Should visiting US President Donald Trump have extended his stay in China a little longer, he would have been able to witness the unbelievable avalanche of online shopping orders that Chinese consumers will make during the Singles Day sales bonanza, which starts on Nov 11.

item.title: A new opportunity to expand Sino-US trade
item.author: 中国日报-观点
item.description: The upcoming meeting between President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump is expected to further deepen China-US cooperation and draw a new map for bilateral ties in the "New Era", which, according to Xi, China has already entered.
